{{Quote|Anyone for an honest game of chance? No? Me neither. Play, and I'll raise you fifty.|Devon Fuller|<ref name="GG9" />}}
'''Devon Fuller''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who worked as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] before joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He helped the Rebels at their [[Ansarra Rebel base]] until it was raided by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Fuller made it out of the base alive and was compensated by the Rebels for the loss of his ship, the ''[[Freebooter]]''.

Devon Fuller posted on [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]] on the [[K4 security droid]] and the [[AD-series weapons maintenance droid|AD armorer droid]] entries.<ref name="CF">''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]''</ref>

==Biography==
Devon Fuller attended, and then was expelled from, the [[Vensenor Flight Academy/Legends|Vensenor Flight Academy]]. Afterwards he worked as a [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] on a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian Corvette]], and later a gem-smuggler. Eventually Devon bought his own freighter, the ''[[Croc of Gold]]'', and became a legitimate trader. His trading was successful due to the help of [[Owen Sareth]], who brought him into the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

Fuller had an impressive [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] criminal dossier, no less than five independent rewards for his capture, and lost two ships in service to the Alliance. The ''Croc of Gold'' was destroyed by Imperials over [[Tashtor Seneca]]. His second ship, the ''[[Freebooter]]'', was wrecked during an attack on the [[Ansarra Rebel base]]. His third ship, ''[[The Solar Flair]]'', was loaned to him by the Rebel Alliance.

While working for the Alliance, Fuller met [[Corwin Shelvay/Legends|Corwin Shelvay]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and fugitive from the Empire. He introduced Shelvay to his Rebel contacts, bringing Shelvay into the Alliance. Shelvay, in turn, convinced Fuller to believe in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="GG9" />

==Personality and traits==
Fuller behaved as if money was his only motivation, and presented himself as an arrogant mercenary and con man, but his work for the Rebel Alliance revealed that he valued freedom more than money, and that he would go a long way to help his few close friends. He was a reckless pilot, and despite losing two ships he showed no sign of being more careful with the ''Solar Flair''. [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]] was aware of Fuller, and considered him "strong in the [[The Force/Legends|Force]]".<ref name="GG9" />

He often wore a battered [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain's]] cap, a trophy from his career, along with a worn flight jacket and steelcloth heavy duty trousers, making him look more like a starfighter pilot than a freighter captain.<ref name="GG9" />

According to an Imperial Criminal Dossier, Fuller was charged with the following crimes against the Galactic Empire:<ref name="GG9" />
*Accomplice to Murder
*Aiding and Abetting Known Traitors
*Aggression Against a Member of the Imperial Armed Forces
*Assault of an Imperial Officer
*Assault with Intent to Injure
*Breach of the Peace
*Breaking into an Imperial Installation
*Breaking Out of an Imperial Installation
*Carrying a Concealed Weapon
*Carrying an Illegal Weapon
*Conspiracy
*Drunk and Disorderly Conduct
*Failure to Obey the Imperial Navy
*Falsifying Documents
*Forgery
*[[Treason/Legends|High Treason]]
*Illegal Gambling
*Impersonating an Imperial Officer
*Jamming Imperial Communications
*Membership in an Illegal Organization
*Murder
*Obstructing the Court of Justice
*Operating an Illegally Modified Starship
*Operating an Unregistered Starship
*Piloting a Repulsorlift Vehicle Without Due Care and Attention
*Piloting a Starship Without Due Care
*Possession of Stolen Goods
*Reckless Endangerment
*Resisting Arrest
*Sabotage
*Smuggling
*Theft
*Transportation of Illegal Aliens
*Transportation of Stolen Goods
*Transportation of Restricted Items
*Trespassing on Imperial Property
*Trespassing on Private Property
*Unauthorized Review of Imperial Data Files
*Violation of Imperial Customs Regulations
*Willful Destruction of Imperial Property
